justification: The diff successfully migrates from JUnit 4 to JUnit 5 (Jupiter) and updates Maven compiler settings to Java 21, but it doesn't leverage any Java 21-specific idioms (records, pattern matching, virtual threads, etc.). The migration of JUnit 4 annotations (@Before, @After, @Test) to JUnit 5 (@BeforeEach, @AfterEach, @Test) and assertions (Assert.assertEquals to Assertions.assertEquals) is correct and complete. The pom.xml updates are coherent, including the exclusion of JUnit 4 from allure-junit4 and the addition of junit-jupiter. However, the diff is purely a framework migration without Java 21 language feature adoption.
